content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 29, 1989

David Cameron
2100-A G Street
Sacramento, CA 95816

RE: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-89-691

Dear Mr. Cameron:

You have asked several questions regarding where to file
major donor reports required under the Political Reform Act.¹
Since your letter requests only general information, we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

DISCUSSION

To determine where to file major donor campaign statements,
you must first determine whether you qualify as a "state,"
"county" or "city" major donor committee. These are distinguished
as follows:

"State" major donor committees are committees primarily
formed to make contributions or independent expenditures to
support or oppose state candidates or measures or any number
of candidates or measures being voted upon in more than one
county.

"County" major donor committees are committees primarily
formed to make contributions or independent expenditures to
support or oppose candidates or measures being voted upon in
a single county election or in any number of jurisdictions
wholly within a single county.

_______________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2 Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

"City" major donor committees are committee which make
contributions or independent expenditures to support or
oppose candidates or measures being voted upon in a single
city.

Major donor committee campaign statements are required to be
filed as follows:

State Major Donor Committees:

(1) The original and one copy with the Secretary of State;
(2) Two copies with the Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles
    County;
(3) Two copies with the Registrar of Voters of the City and
    county of San Francisco.
(4) Two copies with the clerk of the county in which the
    committee is domiciled*

B. County Major Donor Committees:

(1) The original and one copy with the clerk of the county
    in which the committee is active;
(2) Two copies with the clerk of the county in which the
    committee is domiciled.*

C. City Major Donor Committee:

(1) The original and one copy with the clerk of the city.

*If the committee address is outside California, Los Angeles
will be considered the county of domicile.

As noted above, a major donor committee which has a non-
California address must use Los Angeles County as their county of
domicile. (Section 84215.) Additionally, a major donor committee
which qualifies as a state major donor committee but also makes
contributions to city or county candidates or measures is not
required to file reports where a city or county major donor
committee is required to file. However, a city or county major
donor committee which later during a calendar year qualifies as another type
of committee (e.g., a city committee becomes a county or state
committee) must continue to file copies of its campaign statement
in the original city or county, in addition to the other locations
in which it is required to file. (Section 84215(g).)

Additionally, no more than the original and one copy, or two
copies, of a campaign statement must be filed with any one county
or city clerk or with the Secretary of State. (Section 84215(f).)

Since major donor committees automatically terminate at the
end of each calendar year, and must requalify as a new committee
before they are required to file campaign statements in any
subsequent year, a prior year's filing requirement does not affect
the current year's filing requirements.

I have enclosed the FPPC "Informational Manual on Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act for Major Donor
Committees and Independent Expenditure Committees." However,
please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have
additional questions.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[illegible signature]

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

David Cameron
2100-A G Street
Sacramento, CA 95816

DEC 12 9 50 AM '89

December 11, 1989

Fair Political Practice Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Gentlepersons:

If an individual, etc. contributes $10,000 or more in
California elections they qualify as a Major Donor if they
are not already filing as a PAC, etc. I have several
questions about where to file Major Donor reports.

If a non-Californian qualifies as a Major Donor for the
first time by giving to one election in one county, where do
they file?

If a non-Californian qualifies as a Major Donor for the
first time by giving to one election that is in 2 counties,
where do they file?

If a non-Californian qualifies as a Major Donor for the
first time by giving to several elections in several
counties, where do they file?

If a Californian from one county qualifies as a Major Donor
for the first time by giving to one election within a county
different from their own, where do they file?

If a non-Californian had filed a Major Donor statement in a
previous year in one county and now contributes $10,000 to
an election in another county, where do they file?

If non-Californian qualifies as a Major Donor for the first
time by contributing to a statewide initiative campaign,
where do they file? Also if they had filed Major Donor
statement previously does that have any bearing?

If a Californian residing in one county qualifies by giving
to a statewide initiative in another county, where do they
file?

Sincerely,

David Cameron

December 14, 1989


David Cameron
2100-A G Street
Sacramento, CA  95816

                                    Re:  Letter No. 89-691

Dear Mr. Cameron:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on December 12, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                    Very truly yours,


                                    [illegible]
                                    Jeanne Pritchard
                                    Chief Technical Assistance
                                      and Analysis Division

428 I Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

sections.question: You have asked several questions regarding where to file major donor reports required under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion: Major donor committee campaign statements are required to be filed as follows: State Major Donor Committees file with the Secretary of State, Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder, San Francisco Registrar of Voters, and the county clerk of domicile. County Major Donor Committees file with the county clerk where active and the county clerk of domicile. City Major Donor Committees file with the city clerk. Committees with non-California addresses use Los Angeles County as domicile.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks where major donor committees must file their required campaign statements under the Political Reform Act, seeking guidance on filing locations based on committee type.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provides detailed filing location requirements that vary based on whether the committee qualifies as a state, county, or city major donor committee, with specific instructions for multi-jurisdictional committees and non-California domiciled committees.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document includes both a December 14 acknowledgment letter and the December 29 substantive response. Marked as informal assistance rather than formal written advice. Two distinct letters compiled in single document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked several questions regarding where to file major donor reports required under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Major donor committee campaign statements are required to be filed as follows: State Major Donor Committees file with the Secretary of State, Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder, San Francisco Registrar of Voters, and the county clerk of domicile. County Major Donor Committees file with the county clerk where active and the county clerk of domicile. City Major Donor Committees file with the city clerk. Committees with non-California addresses use Los Angeles County as domicile.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC informal advice response to David Cameron regarding the filing locations for major donor committee campaign statements under the Political Reform Act, providing detailed guidance on where different types of major donor committees must file their reports.
